skill.name: changelog-generator
skill.description: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing commit history, categorizing changes, and transforming technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Turns hours of manual changelog writing into minutes of automated generation.
skill.summary: Transforms git commit history into clear, user-facing changelogs and release notes.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and Ruby or shell backtick patterns, but review found only markdown prose, metadata, and fenced natural-language examples in SKILL.md and audit_output.json. No executable code, prompt injection, network access, credential access, or bundled command execution was found.

content.user_title: Generate Customer Changelogs From Git Commits
content.value_statement: Release notes take time because technical commits must be translated for customers. This skill groups commit history, removes internal noise, and drafts clear changelogs.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes commit history from a time period, version, or tag range.
Groups changes into features, improvements, fixes, security, and breaking changes.
Rewrites technical commit messages into customer-facing release notes.
Filters internal work such as tests, refactors, and maintenance changes.
Formats output as structured markdown for changelog or release pages.
Applies custom style guidance from a user-provided changelog guide.
limitations: It depends on available commit data and the clarity of commit messages.
It does not create releases, tags, or repository changes by itself.
Large histories may need a narrower date or version range.
Generated notes should be reviewed before customer publication.

use_cases.title: Prepare launch notes
use_cases.description: Turn engineering commits into customer-ready notes for a version launch or weekly product update.

use_cases.title: Maintain public changelogs
use_cases.description: Draft readable CHANGELOG entries that explain changes without exposing internal development details.

use_cases.title: Summarize release work
use_cases.description: Create internal or external release summaries from recent commits before a deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic changelog
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a first draft from the latest release range.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a changelog from commits since the last release.

prompt_templates.title: Weekly update
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for recurring product update posts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a user-facing changelog for all commits from the past week.

prompt_templates.title: Version release notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing notes for a named release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create release notes for version 2.5.0. Group changes by features, improvements, fixes, and breaking changes.

prompt_templates.title: Styled changelog
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when your team has a specific voice or changelog format.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a changelog for commits since v2.4.0 using the guidelines in CHANGELOG_STYLE.md. Remove internal maintenance commits.

output_examples.input: Create a changelog for commits from the past 7 days.
output: New Features: Team Workspaces let users organize separate projects and invite collaborators.
Improvements: Sync is faster, and search now includes file contents.
Fixes: Large image uploads, timezone display, and notification counts were corrected.
Highlights: The release adds keyboard shortcuts and improves project navigation.
Quality: Upload handling is more reliable for large files.
Compatibility: No breaking changes were identified in the reviewed commits.

best_practices: Provide a clear commit range, tag range, or release version.
Share style guidance when the changelog must match a brand voice.
Review the draft for accuracy before publishing it to customers.
anti_patterns: Generating notes from an unlimited repository history.
Publishing generated release notes without product or engineering review.
Expecting clear customer copy from vague or inconsistent commit messages.

faq.question: Does this skill run git commands automatically?
faq.answer: No bundled executable code is included. The user or host tool must provide commit information or allow repository inspection.

faq.question: Can it use my team's changelog format?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your format rules or a changelog style file, and ask the skill to follow them.

faq.question: What commit ranges can it summarize?
faq.answer: It can work from dates, release tags, versions, or any commit list you provide.

faq.question: Will it include internal maintenance changes?
faq.answer: It is designed to filter tests, refactors, and maintenance work unless you ask to include them.

faq.question: Can it create GitHub releases?
faq.answer: It can draft the release notes text, but it does not publish releases by itself.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code in the marketplace metadata.
